# Pavel Konrád — Technical Sound Designer

> Technical sound designer based in Prague, Czech Republic. Specialises in adaptive
> audio for interactive media and games: sound creation in Ableton Live, event design
> in FMOD Studio, and implementation in Unity (C#). Ten years in music (jazz
> conservatory) plus a software-development background (C#, Ruby, Python). Open to
> freelance, contract, or full-time — remote or on-site. Happy to do a test task.

## Contact
- Email: audio@pavelkonrad.cz
- Location: Prague, Czech Republic (open to remote across the EU)
- Portfolio: https://pavelkonrad.cz

## Looking for
Roles in game / interactive audio: Technical Sound Designer, Sound Designer, Audio
Designer, or audio implementer. Freelance, contract, or full-time. Remote or on-site.

## Skills
- Audio middleware: FMOD Studio (event design, state-based mixing, parameters, banks); working knowledge of Wwise
- DAW & sound creation: Ableton Live (Drift synthesis, sampling, mixing, mastering), Cubase, Reaper, Pro Tools; field recording
- Game engines: Unity 6 — URP, C# scripting, design patterns (state machine), audio integration
- Programming: C#, Ruby, Python
- Music: jazz harmony, production, arrangement, 10+ years live performance

## Selected work
- [JINGI case study](https://pavelkonrad.cz/jingi): 2026 Unity dice-game prototype. Full game audio + C# game logic; adaptive audio in FMOD driven by a state machine; all sounds from scratch via synthesis and field recording (no stock samples). Gameplay: https://youtu.be/seyel3vAcRI
- Boreal Flow (2025): interactive audio-photographic exhibition in Unity/FMOD. https://youtu.be/Qq6WqQtcyCg — build: https://pavelkonrad.itch.io/artic-journey-boreal-flow

## Education
- Masaryk University, Faculty of Arts — BA, Theory of Interactive Media (2021–2024)
- Jaroslav Ježek Conservatory — DiS., Music / Jazz (2004–2011)

## Languages
- Czech (native), English (B2)
